name: Rufus Harley
abstract: Rufus Harley Jr. (May 20, 1936 – August 1, 2006) was an American jazz musician known primarily as the first jazz musician to adopt the Great Highland bagpipe as his primary instrument.

about_quotes.speaker: Joel Dorn
about_quotes.evidence: And he distributed some of the independent records I had made with Duke Pearson and Sonny Stitt and people like that, and like a jazz bagpipe player from Philly named Rufus Harley.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joel Dorn interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Dawoud Sabu Adeyola
about_quotes.evidence: And then Rufus Harley used to come to Buff — you ever heard of Rufus Harley? ... Rufus would come to town and he’d come up... So we have people like this you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dawoud Sabu Adeyola interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 30, 2025
